title: HDFC Bank Workforce Drops by Over 3,300 as Operations Automated
summary: HDFC Bank Ltd.’s workforce declined by 3,343 employees in the last financial year as India’s largest private lender accelerated efforts to automate operations and redeploy staff to customer-facing roles.

title: SK Hynix Shares Plunge Most on Record in Deepening Korea Selloff
summary: South Korea’s AI-fueled stock rally came under renewed pressure Monday as SK Hynix Inc. tumbled by a record 15%, underscoring growing investor concerns that the boom has become overstretched.

title: US and Iran Trade Strikes, Dispute Whether Hormuz Is Open
summary: The US and Iran exchanged fresh strikes overnight into Monday, prolonging a spate of tit-for-tat attacks at a time when the two nations offer conflicting statements over whether the Strait of Hormuz is open to shipping.

title: Oil Rallies as Conflict Over Hormuz Escalates With Fresh Strikes
summary: Oil gained after the US carried out another wave of strikes against Iran, with the two sides disputing whether the Strait of Hormuz was open.

title: Shein Is Said to Target Up to $3 Billion Hong Kong IPO by August
summary: Shein Global Holdings Ltd. is seeking to list in Hong Kong as soon as in August after securing approval from China’s securities regulator, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Saylor’s Strategy Raises Reserve to $3 Billion With Stock Sale
summary: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. raised about $467 million through the sale of common stock, lifting its cash reserve to roughly $3 billion while refraining from buying or selling Bitcoin.

title: Chip Selloff Hits Stocks as Oil Gains Lift Yields: Markets Wrap
summary: A selloff in South Korean chipmakers drove global technology stocks lower, while oil and bond yields climbed after the weekend’s round of strikes and counterattacks in the Middle East.

title: The C-Beauty Industry’s Unique Advantage
summary: As Korean entertainment helped turn K-beauty into a global phenomenon, China's growing influence in popular culture could do the same for its cosmetics industry, Bloomberg Opinion columnist Juliana Liu says. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Kenya Eyes $500 Million Eurobond Buyback to Ease Debt Pressure
summary: Kenya is considering buying back as much as $500 million of its eurobonds to extend maturities and ease repayment pressures, according to people familiar with the plans.

title: Disney Exiting Streaming Could Spur 40% Rally, Wells Fargo Says
summary: Walt Disney Co. shares have been in a slump for years, but Wells Fargo Securities said there’s one possible move that could reverse the trend: ditching its streaming-video business.

title: VW CEO Outlines Up to 50,000 More Job Cuts to Hit Savings Goals
summary: Volkswagen AG Chief Executive Officer Oliver Blume outlined a plan to eliminate as many as 50,000 more jobs globally as he attempts a far-reaching overhaul to reduce costs at Europe’s biggest carmaker.

title: US Stock Futures Fall as Mideast Tensions Flare Anew, Oil Climbs
summary: US stocks dropped before the bell on Monday as investors assessed tit-for-tat attacks between the US and Iran and conflicting information over the status of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Oil Rises as US, Iran Dispute Whether Hormuz is Open; Big Week in Markets | Bloomberg Brief 07/13/26
summary: Oil gains after the US and Iran trade fresh strikes. The two sides are issuing conflicting declarations whether the Strait of Hormuz is open. Investors prepare for Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh's testimony on Capitol Hill this week along with CPI and Wall Street earnings. Thierry Wizman of  Macquarie looks ahead to the busy week. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Wall Street Mounts Pushback on Trillion-Dollar Stablecoin Boom
summary: For years, banks largely watched stablecoins evolve from a niche cryptocurrency product into a payments network moving tens of trillions of dollars annually. Now they’re dusting off the same collaborative playbook that produced Zelle, betting shared infrastructure is the best way to stop digital dollars from encroaching further on their business.

title: Russian Refinery Runs Plunge to Lowest in More Than Two Decades
summary: A wave of Ukrainian attacks has pushed Russian refining runs to the lowest in more than 21 years, deepening a domestic fuel crunch and further squeezing the global market.

title: Bangladesh Is a Nuclear Power Test Case for Developing Nations
summary: Atomic energy offers emerging economies a way to wean themselves off imported fossil fuels, but it also has downsides.

title: Renewed Iran Woes Flip Script for Traders Betting War Risks Over
summary: A day after making an investment call tied to subsiding Middle East tensions, BCA Research’s Marko Papic had to scrap his recommendation at a loss. Donald Trump had said the war in Iran was back on.

title: US & Iran Trade Fresh Strikes, SK Hynix Shares Fall Most on Record | The Opening Trade 7/13/2026
summary:
Oil gained after the US carried out another wave of strikes against Iran, with the two sides disputing whether the Strait of Hormuz was open. Global benchmark Brent earlier approached $80, before paring some of the increase. The US on Sunday struck dozens of targets to degrade Iran’s ability to attack international shipping through the waterway. Iran said the strait would be closed “until further notice,” though Western navies insisted it remained open.

South Korea’s AI-fueled stock rally came under renewed pressure Monday as SK Hynix  tumbled by a record 15%, underscoring growing investor concerns that the boom had become overstretched. 

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards and Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: India Trade Deficit Widens as Hormuz Risks Cloud Outlook
summary: India’s trade deficit widened in June, with uncertainty over the US-Iran conflict and shipping through the Strait of Hormuz continuing to cloud the outlook for global supply chains.

title: Chile Debt Sale Window Opens as Investors Ignore Mounting Risks
summary: Chile’s government has a window of opportunity to sell debt abroad as sovereign spreads hover near two-decade lows, with investors shrugging off an escalation in the Middle East conflict, domestic debt woes and a stagnating economy.

title: Indonesia Avoids S&P Rating Outlook Cut in Win for Prabowo
summary: Indonesia retained its investment-grade rating and stable outlook at S&P Global Ratings, a reprieve for President Prabowo Subianto and his fiscal policy credibility after a bond market selloff in June.

title: Investors Expect ‘Sunshine and Rainbows’ This Earnings Season
summary: Estimates are running up against sticky inflation and rising odds of interest rate hikes. With stocks near records, there’s little room for error.

title: Fed’s Bowman Urges Global Financial Watchdog to be Flexible
summary: The Federal Reserve’s top bank cop warned the Financial Stability Board against enforcing “strict rules” that are not suitable for a diverse group of countries, saying those efforts can erode the international watchdog’s effectiveness.

title: Korea Selloff Deepens as SK Hynix Plunges Most on Record | The Pulse 7/13/2026
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Seema Shah, Principal Asset Management Chief Global Strategist; Cassander Verwey, JPMorgan EMEA Head of M&A  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Germany’s Smag Sinks in Debut as Defense Stock Appetite Ebbs
summary: Smag Mobile Antenna Masts AG shares tumbled as much as 33% below the initial public offering price in their German trading debut, marking the latest sign of waning demand for defense stocks in Europe.

title: Traders Grapple With World That’s Good for Dollar, Bad for Bonds
summary: What’s good for the US dollar isn’t always good for US bonds — but investors are finding ways to work around it.

title: UAE Steps Up Moves to Help Term Buyers Avoid Hormuz Transits
summary: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. is broadening the options for long-term customers to take offshore oil from outside the Strait of Hormuz, stepping up efforts that enable buyers to circumvent the chokepoint.

title: Apple’s $600 Billion Rally Fueled by Traders Fleeing AI Selloff
summary: Investors are flocking back to Apple Inc. as nervousness about artificial intelligence spending weighs on the stocks of chipmakers and cloud-computing giants.

title: Maker of Soya Supreme Cooking Oil Plans Pakistan IPO This Month
summary: Cooking oil manufacturer Agro Processors & Atmospheric Gases Ltd. is planning an initial public offering in Pakistan this month to finance a plant expansion, adding to a record year for listings in the country.

title: CLO Fund’s 50% Tumble Fuels Power Struggle That Spills Into Open
summary: A fight over who gets to run a $580 million credit fund has erupted into an unusually public Wall Street brawl, pitting claims of poor performance against accusations of a cash grab ahead of a proxy vote later this month.
